audienceSections.normal: In a plan to shop at the market, you will use [[grammar:On va + infinitif]]. Use [[grammar:On va]] followed by an infinitive for a shared plan. On va acheter des tomates means ‘We are going to buy tomatoes.’ Do not confuse this with on va au marché, where au marks a destination.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter presents [[grammar:On va + infinitif]] in a plan to shop at the market. Conversational on has first-person plural reference. Finite va plus a bare infinitive forms the aller periphrasis; va au marché instead contains the contracted destination phrase à + le.

audienceSections.normal: Use [[grammar:On va]] followed by an infinitive for a shared plan. On va acheter des tomates means ‘We are going to buy tomatoes.’ Do not confuse this with on va au marché, where au marks a destination.
audienceSections.expert: Conversational on has first-person plural reference. Finite va plus a bare infinitive forms the aller periphrasis; va au marché instead contains the contracted destination phrase à + le.

audienceSections.normal: Read the French aloud in short groups. Learn each noun with its article because the article helps you remember gender.
audienceSections.expert: The examples use contemporary standard French with informal singular tu. Article-bearing citation forms preserve lexical gender, including explicit gender marking where elision hides it.

breakdown.normal:
Read each exact french line, then check its natural English meaning. Watch for [[grammar:On va + infinitif]] where it appears.

- On va au marché demain ? — Shall we go to the market tomorrow?
- Oui, on va acheter des tomates. — Yes, we are going to buy tomatoes.
- Je veux aussi des fruits. — I also want some fruit.
- D'accord, le marché est près du parc. — Okay, the market is near the park.
- On part le matin ? — Shall we leave in the morning?
- Oui, à neuf heures. — Yes, at nine o'clock.
breakdown.expert:
These exact reading lines provide the distributional evidence for [[grammar:On va + infinitif]]. The grammar section above analyzes the relevant morphology and syntax.

- On va au marché demain ? — Shall we go to the market tomorrow?
- Oui, on va acheter des tomates. — Yes, we are going to buy tomatoes.
- Je veux aussi des fruits. — I also want some fruit.
- D'accord, le marché est près du parc. — Okay, the market is near the park.
- On part le matin ? — Shall we leave in the morning?
- Oui, à neuf heures. — Yes, at nine o'clock.
